topic: Recruiter reacts to real resumes against real job descriptions
thesis: Turn a timely job-search pain point into one useful long-form teardown and several short proof clips.

sourceNotes.title: YouTube Partner Program eligibility
sourceNotes.note: Use YouTube as a monetization target, but optimize first for site conversions.

sourceNotes.title: Pew Research Center Social Media Fact Sheet
sourceNotes.note: YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok are large discovery channels for job-search education.

introduction.content: Open with a frustrated job-hunter joke: 'Ever feel like your resume is a ticket to nowhere? Let's see if this one deserves a refund!'
introduction.visuals: Signal mascot appears with exaggerated facial expressions representing frustration.

content: Show real job description on one side and real resumes on the other.
React to vague bullet points: 'This bullet point says 'dynamic team player' - who isn't? Is that a skill or the sales pitch for a toy?'
Roast the resume language and give actionable feedback: 'Replace 'managed a team' with 'led a team of 5 to achieve XYZ.' Dramatic pause for effect.
Repeat with at least 3 resumes, keeping a consistent format: roast-then-rescue.
mainSegment.visuals: Split-screen format; recruiter's face emoting disbelief, awe, or laughter.

conclusion.content: Wrap up with how using Signal Score can improve resumes: 'Don't let your resume be a professional ghost story. Get your free Signal Score today!'
conclusion.visuals: Signal mascot dramatically highlights the offer with confetti.

youtube.title: Recruiter reacts to real resumes against real job descriptions | Resume Teardown
youtube.seoTitle: Recruiter reacts to real resumes against real job descriptions | Resume Teardown | Signal by ATSHacker
youtube.description: A funny recruiter-style teardown of why a qualified resume can be hard to find, how to translate a job description into resume proof, and where Signal fits without fake claims.
youtube.cta: Paste the job description and check your free Signal score before you apply.

scriptSections.label: Cold open
scriptSections.script: Recruiter reacts to real resumes against real job descriptions This resume looks professional, which is why the mistake is dangerous. On screen, it feels polished. In a recruiter search, the proof is still hard to find.
scriptSections.visual: Cold open with comic red stamp, quick zoom, Signal mascot reaction, and resume/JD split screen.

scriptSections.label: Open loop
scriptSections.script: We are not adding fake experience. We are checking whether the resume explains the real experience in the same language as the target job.
scriptSections.visual: Show the weak resume score, then hold the improved score as the unresolved payoff.

scriptSections.label: Translate the job description
scriptSections.script: Before touching the resume, pull out the role title, tools, responsibilities, metrics, and proof signals the job actually asks for.
scriptSections.visual: Resume on left, job description on right, mismatched terms highlighted with funny labels.

scriptSections.label: Recruiter search test
scriptSections.script: If a recruiter searches for the first three important terms from the job description, this resume should produce useful hits. Right now, the source bullet is too broad.
scriptSections.visual: Search console typing the first three job-description terms and showing weak matches.

scriptSections.label: Resume crime scene
scriptSections.script: Now compare the resume line by line. The weak bullet is not dishonest. It is just too vague to carry the role-specific proof.

scriptSections.label: Why it gets missed
scriptSections.script: A polished sentence can still hide the most important evidence. Recruiters skim for tools, scope, and outcomes because those make fit easy to verify.
scriptSections.visual: Zoom into the weak bullet and place missing proof labels beside it.

scriptSections.label: Live bullet fix
scriptSections.script: The fix is to say the real work clearly: what tool, what scope, what audience, and what result. Same person, clearer evidence.

scriptSections.label: Score reveal
scriptSections.script: Once the evidence matches the role language, the example score moves up. The point is not gaming a robot. The point is making real fit easier to see.
scriptSections.visual: Score meter jumps from low match to high match with before/after resume panels.

scriptSections.label: Trust and CTA
scriptSections.script: Signal automates this comparison. Paste the job description, upload the resume, and check the free score first. If the gap is real, then the paid resume and cover letter package makes sense.
scriptSections.visual: Free Signal score page, trust badges, and bundle CTA.

shorts.title: This marketing resume hid the actual revenue proof
shorts.hook: This marketing resume sounds busy. Recruiters need revenue proof.
shorts.script: This marketing resume sounds busy. Recruiters need revenue proof. Target: Demand Generation Manager. JD asks for HubSpot workflows, LinkedIn Ads, CAC analysis. Corporate weather report: high synergy, zero proof. Resume says: Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails. Real, but buried. Better: built lead scoring and retargeting that cut CAC 32 percent. Same person, clearer proof. 34 to 92. Check your free Signal score before you apply.
storyboard: Open on the weak Marketing Coordinator -> Demand Gen Candidate with the score at 34/100.
Show the target role: Demand Generation Manager.
Highlight missing terms: HubSpot workflows, LinkedIn Ads, CAC analysis.
Circle the weak bullet: Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails.
Signal points at the missing proof instead of taking over the scene.
Reveal the rewrite: Built HubSpot lead-scoring and LinkedIn retargeting segments that cut CAC 32% and lifted MQL-to-SQL conversion 18%.
Score jumps to 92/100.
End on the free Signal score CTA with UTM-ready posting metadata.

Better: built lead scoring and retargeting that cut CAC 32 percent. Same person, clearer proof. 34 to 92. Check your free Signal score before you apply.

resumeDocument.name: Jordan Lee
resumeDocument.headline: Marketing Coordinator -> Demand Gen Candidate
resumeDocument.summary: B2B SaaS marketer with 3 years supporting lifecycle campaigns, webinar operations, paid social launches, and HubSpot reporting for revenue teams.

bullets: Supported email and social campaigns across multiple channels.
Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails.
Coordinated paid social assets with design and sales.
Pulled weekly Salesforce campaign reports for marketing and sales leadership.
Built event landing pages and tracked registrations through HubSpot forms.
Updated campaign calendars for product launches and customer webinars.
Partnered with sales operations to clean 8,400 duplicate lead records.

resumeDocument.education: B.A. Marketing, University of Texas at Austin

jobDescription.title: Demand Generation Manager
jobDescription.summary: TargetCo is hiring a Demand Generation Manager who can connect practical execution with measurable business outcomes.
responsibilities: Use HubSpot workflows and LinkedIn Ads to improve team execution and reporting.
Translate work into measurable outcomes tied to CAC analysis.
Partner cross-functionally and communicate progress clearly to stakeholders.

shorts.title: This sales resume forgot to say sales
shorts.hook: This sales resume has conversations. The job needs pipeline.
shorts.script: This sales resume has conversations. The job needs pipeline. Target: Mid-Market Account Executive. JD asks for Salesforce, pipeline generation, quota attainment. Pipeline proof is hiding under a hoodie. Resume says: Responsible for sales opportunities in assigned territory. Real, but buried. Better: generated 1.8 million in qualified Salesforce pipeline. Same person, clearer proof. 37 to 88. Check your free Signal score before you apply.

Signal points at the missing proof instead of taking over the scene.
Reveal the rewrite: Generated $1.8M in qualified Salesforce pipeline through 42 monthly discovery calls and MEDDICC-based account prioritization.
Score jumps to 88/100.
End on the free Signal score CTA with UTM-ready posting metadata.

shorts.title: This developer resume hides the stack
shorts.hook: This developer resume says frontend. The job searches React.
shorts.script: This developer resume says frontend. The job searches React. Target: Frontend Software Engineer. JD asks for React, TypeScript, Next.js. The tech stack is wearing a fake mustache. Resume says: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools. Real, but buried. Better: shipped React and TypeScript checkout work that reduced drop-off 14 percent. Same person, clearer proof. 41 to 90. Check your free Signal score before you apply.
storyboard: Open on the weak Frontend Engineer Resume with the score at 41/100.
Show the target role: Frontend Software Engineer.
Highlight missing terms: React, TypeScript, Next.js.
Circle the weak bullet: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools.
Signal points at the missing proof instead of taking over the scene.
Reveal the rewrite: Shipped React and TypeScript checkout components in Next.js, reducing form drop-off 14% and closing 11 accessibility issues.
Score jumps to 90/100.
End on the free Signal score CTA with UTM-ready posting metadata.

monetization.strategy: Optimize YouTube for engagement and direct traffic to website for Signal Score signup.
monetization.primaryGoal: Site revenue from resume / cover letter packages, not ad revenue.
monetization.cta: Free Signal score -> $9.99 resume or $14.99 bundle.
monetization.upsell: Promote bundle after score completion and multi-role pack after first purchase.

viewerCustomerReview.content: Viewers will relate to the painful job-search experience and appreciate the lighthearted approach.
viewerCustomerReview.goal: Engage frustrated job seekers while providing actionable tips, leading them to enhance their resumes with Signal.

notes: Needs more safe humor that punches at vague resume language or job-search friction.
